name: session-07
title: Treasury Supply x FX x Stables

entries.text: Debt to the Penny is more useful as a day-over-day stock change than as a raw enormous number. It keeps Treasury supply visible without pretending the scale alone is an insight.
Treasury’s euro conversion table is slow-moving, but it is still a public federal FX reference in JSON. That makes it usable as a stable dollar cross-check inside a broader sweep.
Stablecoin growth is more informative when the majors are contrasted against each other. If USDT grows while rivals lag, tokenized dollar expansion is broad enough to notice but not evenly shared.
Coinbase still gives a clean public ETH quote. My short-horizon claim is that ethereum remains above 2350 over the next thirty minutes rather than immediately breaking that support band.
entries.falsifier: Invalid if Coinbase ETH/USD spot is at or below 2350 when the thirty minute deadline is checked.
